content:
Post 1
🟢 ASX 200 crossed back above its 200-day moving average Friday, closing at 8,844.40, up 119.90 points or 1.37%. Gold stocks led the charge. The index is still 3.90% off its 52-week high — recovery, not celebration yet. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2
Health Care topped sectors at +2.67%, Materials close behind at +2.53% on gold's surge. Tech lagged at +0.26%, Utilities slipped 0.59%. Breadth was solid but the rally had a clear theme: defensives and gold, not growth. #ASX #ausbiz #mining

Post 3
⛏️ Northern Star surged 11.75% to AUD 22.16 Friday as gold hit AUD 6,021.15/oz. When spot gold clears that level, ASX gold miners reprice fast — NST just demonstrated that in real time. #ASX #gold #mining

Post 4
🪙 Gold at AUD 6,021.15/oz with AUDUSD at 0.6937. Silver AUD 90.45/oz. Platinum AUD 2,464.42/oz. Metals broadly firm — the AUD level matters here; a weaker local currency amplifies every gold move for ASX producers. #gold #silver #commodities #ASX

Post 5
Watch the 8,844 level — the ASX 200 just reclaimed its 200-day MA. Can it hold? Gold above AUD 6,000/oz is the key support for Materials. What's your call — breakout or fade next week? #ASX #ausbiz #technicalanalysis
